nodes.title: Services are separate concept from processes
nodes.rationale: Services may be processes, external systems, or anything an agent is aware of. ServiceRegistry holds ServiceEntry referencing agent PID, external endpoint, or container ID.

nodes.title: Agent backend selection: all approaches, built iteratively
nodes.rationale: Explicit > manifest > policy > default (Native) fallback chain. Progressive disclosure across K-phases.

nodes.title: Bake SpawnBackend API now, implement incrementally
nodes.rationale: Crystallize config hooks in K2 so K3+ phases are additive, not breaking.

nodes.title: Layered protocol: kernel IPC -> ServiceApi -> protocol adapters
nodes.rationale: API surface that protocols bind to. Shell, MCP, gRPC, HTTP adapters bind to ServiceApi.

nodes.title: Kernel-native first, then A2A + MCP as adapters
nodes.rationale: Internal API surface IS the foundation for external protocols.

nodes.title: K3 = same-node only; clustering moved to K5
nodes.rationale: Cross-node service discovery is clustering; K5 gets clustering (from K6), K6 becomes deep networking.

nodes.title: Defense-in-depth: both routing-time and handler-time gate checks
nodes.rationale: A2ARouter gets routing-time gate check; GovernanceGate remains at handler-time.

nodes.title: ExoChain-stored immutable API contracts
nodes.rationale: Service API schemas are critical artifacts. Immutable chain-anchored schemas are source of truth.

nodes.title: Universal witness with configurable override
nodes.rationale: Sub-microsecond SHAKE-256 hashing; full witnessing by default, opt-out via manifest.

nodes.title: WASM-compiled shell with container sandbox
nodes.rationale: Shell scripts compiled to WASM, hash-chained, run inside container sandbox.

nodes.title: Enable post-quantum signing immediately
nodes.rationale: rvf-crypto already has ML-DSA-65 DualKey; just not called yet.

nodes.title: Chain-agnostic blockchain anchoring trait
nodes.rationale: Define trait surface now, implement simplest binding first.

nodes.title: Zero-knowledge proofs as foundational capability
nodes.rationale: ZK critical for rollups, privacy-preserving governance, verifiable computation.

nodes.title: TEE backend: build compatible, test later
nodes.rationale: TEE = another SpawnBackend variant. Define interface, implement when hardware available.

nodes.title: Maximize adoption of production-ready code, not just RUV
nodes.rationale: Don't build what already exists. Each K-phase starts with integration audit.

nodes.title: CRDT consensus + chain-native ordering
nodes.rationale: Chain provides causal ordering; CRDTs add conflict-free convergence for off-chain state.

nodes.title: Layered routing: tiny-dancer learned + governance enforced
nodes.rationale: tiny-dancer suggests, capabilities verify, governance gate checks, message delivered.

nodes.title: SONA at K5, training data pipeline from K3 forward
nodes.rationale: K3/K4 build training data into all endpoints. SONA reuptake spike in late K4.

nodes.title: Breaking changes required, pre-1.0
nodes.rationale: Services require IPC API evolution. MessageTarget needs Service(name) routing.

nodes.title: Configurable N-dimensional effect algebra
nodes.rationale: Edge devices use 3D, full nodes use 10D. Configuration-driven dimensionality.

nodes.title: K3-K6 iterative cycle, not waterfall
nodes.rationale: Later phases may drive changes that loop back. Symposium minimizes K0-K2 rework.

nodes.title: K6 SPARC spec required before implementation
nodes.rationale: Clear boundary between K5 (apps + clustering) and K6 (networking + replication).

nodes.title: Hierarchical ToolRegistry with kernel base + per-agent overlays
nodes.rationale: Shared base (Arc) + per-agent overlay registries. Resolves CF-3 waste.

nodes.title: Context-based gate actions: generic tool.exec with rich context
nodes.rationale: Pass tool name + effect vector as context; governance handles granularity.

nodes.title: Implement all 25 remaining tools in K4
nodes.rationale: Reference implementations prove the pattern; remaining tools are straightforward.

nodes.title: Wasmtime integration in K4 alongside container runtime
nodes.rationale: WASM container provides same isolation as Docker for WASM modules.

nodes.title: Disk-persisted compiled module cache
nodes.rationale: Wasmtime compilation is 50-200ms; disk persistence survives restarts.

nodes.title: Configurable WASI with read-only sandbox default
nodes.rationale: Per-tool filesystem access via ToolSpec.permissions.

nodes.title: Tree metadata for version history
nodes.rationale: Fast O(1) version queries without chain traversal.

nodes.title: Informational revocation; governance gate handles enforcement
nodes.rationale: Revocation is data; governance rules decide what to do with it.

nodes.title: Central authority + CA chain for tool signing
nodes.rationale: Kernel signs built-ins; developer keypairs with kernel-signed CA for third-party.

nodes.title: Separate ServiceApi and BuiltinTool concepts
nodes.rationale: Tools are one-shot; services are long-running. Different lifecycles.

nodes.title: Routing-time gate deferred to K5 cluster build
nodes.rationale: Single-node handler-time gate is sufficient for K4.

nodes.title: Multi-layer sandboxing: governance + environment + sudo override
nodes.rationale: Three-layer defense in depth: governance, environment config, user override.

nodes.title: WASM snapshots unnecessary now; noted for K6
nodes.rationale: Tools are stateless. Snapshots relevant for long-running WASM services in K5+.

nodes.title: tiny-dancer scoring for native vs WASM routing
nodes.rationale: Governance finds the right route; tiny-dancer finds the best route.

nodes.title: Nervous system model: WeftOS is a cognitive platform
nodes.rationale: Every kernel instance is a node in a distributed nervous system, from ESP32 to Blackwell.

nodes.title: Forest of trees, not one graph (polyglot tree ensemble)
nodes.rationale: Multiple domain-specific structures linked by CrossRefs and Impulses.

nodes.title: Self-calibrating cognitive tick at boot
nodes.rationale: Tick interval hardware-determined, auto-adjusted, advertised to peers.

nodes.title: CRDTs for convergence, Merkle for verification
nodes.rationale: Complementary: CRDTs ensure agreement, Merkle proves honest agreement.

nodes.title: DEMOCRITUS as continuous nervous system operation
nodes.rationale: Not batch; 30-second micro-batches distributed across network.

nodes.title: BLAKE3 forward, SHAKE-256 present
nodes.rationale: New ECC code uses BLAKE3; existing ExoChain keeps SHAKE-256 until K6 migration.

nodes.title: Per-tree scoring with uniform CrossRef indexing
nodes.rationale: Each tree defines own N-dimensional scoring; CrossRef indexing is universal via BLAKE3 hashes.

nodes.title: One feature flag (ecc), boot decides what is active
nodes.rationale: Build provides capability, boot provides configuration.

nodes.title: Selective composition: snow + quinn + selective libp2p
nodes.rationale: Full libp2p too heavy (~80 deps); iroh QUIC-only. Selective composition with tokio control.

nodes.title: Ed25519 public key as node identity
nodes.rationale: No CA needed; node authenticates by signing challenge. Consistent with chain.rs signing.

nodes.title: Noise Protocol for all inter-node encryption (XX/IK)
nodes.rationale: Transport-agnostic, forward secrecy, mutual auth, no X.509.

nodes.title: governance.genesis as cluster trust root
nodes.rationale: Genesis event already exists; its SHAKE-256 hash = cluster ID.

nodes.title: Feature-gated mesh networking (mesh/mesh-discovery/mesh-full)
nodes.rationale: Zero networking code in default build. Follows existing ecc/exochain/cluster pattern.

nodes.title: QUIC primary transport, WebSocket browser fallback
nodes.rationale: QUIC for multiplexing/migration; browsers cannot use QUIC directly.

nodes.title: Ruvector algorithms as pure computation (no I/O)
nodes.rationale: Mesh layer provides I/O; ruvector crates produce/consume messages only.

nodes.title: rvf-wire as mesh wire format
nodes.rationale: Already in workspace, zero-copy deserialization, avoids new serialization dep.

nodes.title: Dual signing (Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65) for cross-node chain events
nodes.rationale: Quantum resistance today + backward compat. Already implemented in chain.rs.

nodes.title: 6-phase implementation (K6.0-K6.5)
nodes.rationale: Each sub-phase independently testable. Prep phase (K6.0) has zero new deps.

nodes.title: Hybrid Noise + ML-KEM-768 post-quantum key exchange
nodes.rationale: Protects against store-now-decrypt-later. ~2.4KB extra per handshake, ~1ms latency.

nodes.title: Layered service resolution with genesis-hash DHT keys
nodes.rationale: 9-step flow: resolution cache, negative cache, connection pool, circuit breaker.

nodes.title: Mesh RPCs reuse ServiceApi adapter pattern
nodes.rationale: MeshAdapter feeds through A2ARouter + ServiceApi. All services remotely callable.

nodes.title: CMVG cognitive sync via multiplexed QUIC streams
nodes.rationale: Chain replication carries ~80% CMVG sync. Dedicated cognitive streams in K7.

nodes.title: Sync framing, stream priority, delta computation, observability
nodes.rationale: RVF wire segments, QUIC priority Chain>Tree>IPC>Cognitive>Impulse, PeerMetrics.

nodes.description: Enriched gate context (tool name + effect vector)
Wasmtime activation + disk cache + WASI scope

nodes.title: Kernel Foundation
nodes.goal: Create clawft-kernel crate with boot, process table, service registry, health, cluster membership

nodes.title: Supervisor + RBAC + ExoChain Integration
nodes.goal: Wire agent supervisor to execute agents, enforce RBAC, integrate with resource tree and chain

nodes.title: Agent-to-Agent IPC
nodes.goal: Implement A2A messaging with typed envelopes, pub/sub topic routing, IPC scope enforcement

nodes.title: Kernel Work-Loop Hardening
nodes.goal: Close 6 gaps: health monitor, watchdog, graceful shutdown, resource tracking, suspend/resume, gate enforcement

nodes.title: Symposium Implementation
nodes.goal: Implement K2 Symposium breaking changes: SpawnBackend, dual signing, IPC restructure, ServiceEntry

nodes.title: WASM Tool Sandboxing / Tool Lifecycle
nodes.goal: 27-tool catalog, 2 reference impls, Build->Deploy->Execute->Version->Revoke lifecycle with ExoChain audit

nodes.title: ECC Integration -- Cognitive Substrate
nodes.goal: Integrate Ephemeral Causal Cognition into kernel: CausalGraph, HnswService, CognitiveTick, CrossRefs, Impulses

nodes.title: Container Integration
nodes.goal: Container-based sidecar service orchestration with Alpine image, Docker integration, Wasmtime activation

nodes.title: Application Framework
nodes.goal: Application manifests, lifecycle management, external framework interop, clustering

nodes.title: Transport-Agnostic Encrypted Mesh Network
nodes.goal: Multi-node cluster with encrypted P2P networking, distributed IPC, chain replication, tree sync, service discovery

nodes.title: OS Gap Filling (Self-Healing + IPC + Content Ops)
nodes.goal: Self-healing supervisor, dead letter queue, named pipes, metrics, logging, artifact store, Weaver engine, config/secret/auth services
